content: Coyote time = brief grace window (~0.1s) after leaving a ledge where a jump still registers. Jump buffering = remembering a jump press made just before landing so it fires on touchdown. Variable jump height = jump force scales with how long the button is held. All three are deliberate physics 'fudges' that make a platformer feel responsive and forgiving rather than punishing; small parameter tweaks have large feel impact. Standard across Mario-style platformers and recommended by Godot/Unity/GameMaker tutorials.
